context.meal: chicken breast strips and hash brown
context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=17; meal=chicken breast strips and hash brown; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=1 min; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=fast; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or other foods intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed cold-served fruit meals used no reheating. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips that are not cold-served fruit or dessert THEN 1 min BECAUSE an earlier personal savory meal used light reheating. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meal intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE working fruit meals matched the broader cold-food pattern. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, broccoli, breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, chicken breast strips, or hash brown THEN 2 min or 3 min depending on the meal, with 2 min repeatedly supported in some work meals and 3 min supported in others BECAUSE multiple working savory meals selected stronger reheating than personal meals. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and vegetables THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed social savory meal selected stronger reheating. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed social fruit meal also used no reheating. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken nuggets, or broccoli THEN 3 min BECAUSE TV-in-front meals consistently selected the hottest reheating level. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, broccoli, chicken breast strips, or hash brown THEN 1 min, 2 min, or 3 min depending on the meal, with evidence now spanning light to high reheating and moderate reheating no longer dominant BECAUSE TV-on-right savory meals have shown substantial within-context variability. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit or dessert items with sauce such as bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN 2 min BECAUSE a prior TV-on-right morning meal selected moderate reheating. Overall, reheating depends strongly on both context and food type: cold fruit stays unheated, personal meals stay lighter, work and some social savory meals are warmer, and TV contexts vary from moderate to high with TV-on-right now especially variable.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion or do not consider occlusion depending on the meal BECAUSE one left-laptop work meal explicitly protected the device view while a later left-laptop evening meal did not, showing mixed evidence. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE front-laptop working meals consistently protected the front view while working. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the right-laptop working meal protected visibility on the side of the device. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the observed social meal prioritized keeping the right side visually open. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the earlier social meal prioritized keeping the left side visually open. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the earlier TV meal explicitly protected the front view. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion or do not consider occlusion depending on the meal, with mixed evidence and no stable single choice BECAUSE several TV-on-right meals protected the right-side view while others, including the new savory meal, ignored occlusion. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed personal meal did not prioritize occlusion. Overall, occlusion concern is selective and usually tracks the location of a socially or visually important target, but TV-on-right remains inconsistent.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a slower pace. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods or fruit meals THEN slow BECAUSE both social meals selected the slowest speed. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods with dip available THEN slow BECAUSE the earlier TV meal used the slowest speed. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit/dessert foods such as bananas and brownies with chocolate sauce THEN medium BECAUSE the prior TV-on-right fruit/dessert meal used medium speed. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, broccoli, chicken breast strips, or hash brown THEN medium, slow, or fast depending on the meal, with medium and faster-than-baseline operation now better supported than a purely cautious pace BECAUSE TV-on-right savory meals have included repeated medium selections and now a fast selection, indicating divided-attention meals in this context can favor efficiency. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meal intended to be served cold THEN slow BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal matched the comfort-oriented pace seen in several other work meals. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken breast strips, hash brown, chicken nuggets, broccoli, bite-sized sandwiches, breaded fish bites, or roasted potatoes THEN medium or slow depending on the meal, with medium repeatedly supported in savory work meals and fast reserved for occasional efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts vary between comfort and efficiency more than other settings. Overall, the user generally prefers slow speed, but working contexts and especially TV-on-right savory meals can shift toward more efficient medium or fast motion.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND solid bite-sized foods in general THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE observed personal meals used the reliability-oriented option. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, or cut fruit THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE both social meals selected the reliability-oriented option. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND foods such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken nuggets, broccoli, bananas, brownies, chicken breast strips, or hash brown THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE most TV meals used the reliability-oriented option, including the new TV-on-right savory meal. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND elongated or mouth-opening-sensitive foods such as bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated TV-on-right sandwich meals selected the narrower-bite option. IF working context AND neutral affect AND elongated or mouth-opening-sensitive foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, fruit pieces, or chicken breast strips THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE multiple working meals selected the narrower-bite option for foods where bite shape may matter. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, breaded fish bites, or roasted potatoes THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE several working savory meals matched the broader reliability-oriented pattern. Overall, the user generally prefers perpendicular skewering, but food-geometry-based exceptions favor parallel skewering for narrower bites.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE observed personal meals avoided extra confirmation, consistent with minimizing interaction burden. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE working meals continued to skip confirmation while occupied. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE social meals avoided extra confirmation. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE TV meals also skipped confirmation, including the new TV-on-right meal. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal signaling. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE social meals used multimodal signaling, suggesting a preference for salient cues when another person is present. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE TV-in-front meals used multimodal signaling, consistent with wanting salient cues when attention is partly directed elsewhere. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech or speech + LED depending on the meal, with speech now better supported overall in this context BECAUSE multiple TV-on-right meals, including the new savory meal, used speech-only while others used multimodal cueing. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED or speech depending on the meal, with speech + LED more common overall but speech-only appearing in some efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working meals are usually multimodal but sometimes simplify cues during more automated operation. Overall, multimodal cueing remains the stable preference, with speech-only appearing most often in TV-on-right and some efficiency-oriented work meals.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user has good mouth control and selected this low-effort, user-driven method in a personal setting. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE social meals used intentional mouth-based initiation despite conversational risk, indicating the user often prefers preserving direct timing control in that context. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used intentional mouth-based initiation. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue or open mouth depending on the meal, with autocontinue now clearly better supported overall BECAUSE repeated TV-on-right meals, including the new savory meal, selected automation in this divided-attention context. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth or autocontinue depending on the meal, with open mouth common across many work meals but autocontinue appearing in some efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts show the clearest tradeoff between direct control and reduced interaction burden. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN open mouth or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use; observed selections also avoid button input.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed personal meal used this low-effort but intentional readiness signal for drinking. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE social meals used intentional mouth-based sip initiation. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used intentional mouth-based sip initiation. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue or open mouth depending on the meal, with autocontinue now clearly better supported overall BECAUSE repeated TV-on-right meals, including the new savory meal, selected automated sip initiation in this divided-attention context. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth or autocontinue depending on the meal, with open mouth common across many work meals but autocontinue appearing in some efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts show the clearest shift toward automated flow. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN open mouth or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use; observed selections also avoid button input.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed personal meal used automation for wiping initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE working meals also used automation. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE social meals did the same. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE both TV-in-front and TV-on-right meals also used automation, including the new episode. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal completion cues. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE social meals used multimodal completion cues. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE TV-in-front meals used multimodal completion cues, consistent with wanting salient signals when attention is partly elsewhere. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech or speech + LED depending on the meal, with speech now better supported overall in this context BECAUSE multiple TV-on-right meals, including the new savory meal, used speech-only completion cueing while others used multimodal cueing. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED or speech depending on the meal, with speech + LED more common overall but speech-only appearing in some efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working meals are usually multimodal but sometimes simplify cues during efficiency-oriented operation. Overall, multimodal completion cueing is still the dominant preference, with speech-only simplification appearing most often in TV-on-right and work meals.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected reliable sensor-based completion detection for feeding, and button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal meal used perception-based drink completion and the user has good head control. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE social meals used perception-based drink completion. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used perception-based drink completion. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with perception now somewhat better supported overall in this context BECAUSE the new TV-on-right savory meal selected perception while earlier TV-on-right meals also included autocontinue. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with perception common across many work meals but autocontinue appearing in some automation-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts show the clearest shift toward reduced user signaling. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN perception or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal meal used perception-based wiping completion and the user has good head control. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE social meals used perception-based wiping completion. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used perception-based wiping completion. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with perception now somewhat better supported overall in this context BECAUSE the new TV-on-right savory meal selected perception while earlier TV-on-right meals also included autocontinue. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with perception common across many work meals but autocontinue appearing in some automation-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts show the clearest shift toward reduced user signaling. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN perception or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the observed personal meal kept the robot in place between tasks to reduce meal time. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN yes BECAUSE a front-laptop evening work meal selected retracting, consistent with protecting the front workspace and screen between tasks. IF working context in other observed laptop positions AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE other working meals consistently prioritized efficiency over reducing visual obstruction. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE social meals still preferred not retracting despite side-specific occlusion concerns. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN yes BECAUSE the earlier TV-in-front meal selected retracting, consistent with protecting the user's front view of the screen between tasks. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE all observed TV-on-right meals, including the new one, did not use retracting. Overall, the user usually prioritizes shorter meal flow, but accepts retracting when a front-facing screen or workspace appears especially important.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed personal fruit meal used no dipping. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed social fruit meal also used no dipping. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the earlier social savory meal selected some dipping but reduced amount, consistent with allowing flavor while limiting mess in front of another person. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meals or foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE working fruit meals matched the broader no-dip pattern in work settings. IF working context AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ketchup or tartar sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE working meals with available sauces avoided dipping, consistent with reducing mess while occupied. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ranch dressing, ketchup, or chocolate sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE TV meals, including the dessert-with-sauce meal, avoided dipping, suggesting the user prefers cleaner, lower-mess operation when attention is divided. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND foods without dippable items or sauces such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken breast strips, or hash brown THEN do not dip BECAUSE repeated TV-on-right savory meals also selected no dipping. Overall, the user strongly prefers avoiding dipping except for a limited social-context reduction to less when sauce is available.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a longer wait, suggesting the user wants more time to respond when not rushed by another task. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE social meals used the longer wait, consistent with allowing more time when attention may be shared. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the TV-in-front meal used the longer wait, consistent with allowing time when attention is partly on the screen. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec or 10 sec depending on the meal, with 100 sec previously common but 10 sec now also supported in a more automation-oriented savory meal BECAUSE TV-on-right evidence now shows both a long response window and a shorter efficiency-oriented setting. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meals THEN 100 sec BECAUSE observed working fruit meals supported a longer response window. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken breast strips, hash brown, breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, or some other work meals with more automation-oriented interaction THEN 10 sec or 100 sec depending on the meal BECAUSE working meals show mixed evidence between faster flow and a longer response window. IF working context AND neutral affect AND many other warm savory meals THEN 100 sec BECAUSE several work meals still used the longer wait. Overall, wait-time preference is stable at 100 sec outside efficiency-oriented contexts, while working meals and some TV-on-right savory meals show the clearest shortening to 10 sec.
